title: What benefits do believers receive from Christ at death?
summary: Professing WSC Q. 37 of the Westminster Shorter Catechism, grounded in 2 Corinthians 5:1-9.
description: This household worship moves through Psalm 59:16-17 and Romans 14:8, and professes WSC Q. 37 — “What benefits do believers receive from Christ at death?”, before closing with 2 Corinthians 5:1-9.

elements.text:
16 But I will sing of your strength;
 I will sing aloud of your steadfast love in the morning.
For you have been to me a fortress
 and a refuge in the day of my distress.
17 O my Strength, I will sing praises to you,
 for you, O God, are my fortress,
 the God who shows me steadfast love.

elements.lede: Respond to God’s call to worship him by praying the Lord’s Prayer together:
elements.text: Our Father who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread, and forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us, and lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. For thine is the kingdom and the power, and the glory, forever and ever. Amen.

elements.lede: Take a moment to “test and examine” your thoughts, feelings, and actions of the past day. “Return to the Lord” by confessing your sin (Lam 3:40).
elements.text:
O Lord, our great God, all holy, Father most gracious, filled with mercy and steadfast love, we humbly come before you, for we have preferred the ways of this world to your ways. We have rebelled against your wisdom and have invited trouble. We have rejected your fatherly guidance and have gotten lost altogether.
O Lord, our great God, all holy, filled with awe, Father most gracious, filled with mercy and steadfast love, incline your ear to our troubles. Hear us as we pour out our sorrows to you. Forgive us, not because of our own righteousness, but because of your great mercy in the gift of your Son, Jesus Christ. It is in his name that we pray, for he is our Savior, and the mediator of the covenant of grace.
Amen.
8 For if we live, we live to the Lord, and if we die, we die to the Lord. So then, whether we live or whether we die, we are the Lord's.

elements.title: The Riches of His Wisdom
elements.lyrics:
Oh the depths of the riches and wisdom
Knowledge and truth, hope and glory
How unsearchable perfect and loving
The One who answers to none
From Him and through Him and to Him belong all things
To Him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

elements.text: 1 For we know that if the tent that is our earthly home is destroyed, we have a building from God, a house not made with hands, eternal in the heavens. 2 For in this tent we groan, longing to put on our heavenly dwelling, 3 if indeed by putting it on we may not be found naked. 4 For while we are still in this tent, we groan, being burdened—not that we would be unclothed, but that we would be further clothed, so that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life. 5 He who has prepared us for this very thing is God, who has given us the Spirit as a guarantee.6 So we are always of good courage. We know that while we are at home in the body we are away from the Lord, 7 for we walk by faith, not by sight. 8 Yes, we are of good courage, and we would rather be away from the body and at home with the Lord. 9 So whether we are at home or away, we make it our aim to please him.
The head of the household may offer words of instruction, encouragement, and exhortation.

Make your ways known upon the earth, Lord God, your saving power among the peoples. Renew your Church in holiness and help us to serve you with joy. Guide the leaders of all nations that justice may prevail throughout the world. Let not the needy be forgotten, nor the hope of the poor taken away. Make us instruments of your peace and let your glory be over all the earth. Prayers for ourselves, our families, friends, and the world may be offered as the Lord leads.

O God of the living, on Holy Saturday your Son our Savior descended to the place of the dead: Look with kindness on all of us who wait in hope for liberation from the corruption of sin and death, and give us a share in the glory of the children of God; through Jesus Christ your Son our Lord. 
Amen.

18 The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all.
Amen.
